inline_comments.message:
At least in the past the recommendation was to compact the RUN lines to minimize the # of layers such as:

RUN cp -pr $POLICY_HOME/examples /home/apexuser \
    && chown -R apexuser:apexuser /home/apexuser/* $POLICY_HOME/etc/* \
    && chmod 755 $POLICY_HOME/etc/*

Note also, that may be it would be OK to do :

chown -R apexuser:apexuser $POLICY_HOME

Probably that's preferable, if I recall correctly, the image shows apex-pdp files under $POLICY_HOME shows to be owned for an uid that doesn't have an account, so they cannot be modified.
